notes: Lock chances are percent per doorway SEGMENT (the shared edge between two cells),
keyed by rank and orientation. Values over 100 mean guaranteed-locked at bias 1.
The sim rolls a segment when the first door on it is placed (the real game rolls
lazily on first click); see README 'Known simplifications'.

lock_chance.comment: ew_by_rank: E-W doors within a rank. ns_boundary: N-S doors, keyed by the LOWER rank of the boundary; 'edge' = cols 0/4, 'center' = cols 1-3. Ranks 1-3 are never locked by chance.

bias.comment: Daily lock-bias multiplier: effective chance = base * bias, bias starts at 1. Locked door: bias = min(bias + locked_delta, 1) unless chance was over high_second_roll_above and a second base-chance roll passes. Unlocked door: bias = max(bias + unlocked_delta, 1) unless chance was under low_second_roll_below and a second base-chance roll fails.

always_unlocked_rooms.comment: Doors of these rooms are guaranteed unlocked and bypass the bias system.

security.comment: A freshly placed whitelisted room's doors may spawn as security doors: requires spawned-today < spawn_limit[level], door distance to the Antechamber center <= distance_cutoff (cell centers 10 units apart), a uniform(0, distance_roll_max) roll strictly above that distance, then the per-room chance (forced to 100 on high security). room_door_chance collapses the datamined per-door Left/Forward/Right table to each room's strongest door (confidence: datamined, simplified).

keycard.comment: Chance (percent) of finding the Keycard on first entry to a source room; the card is permanent for the day and never consumed. Source list from the wiki's spawn locations restricted to rooms modeled in the sim; the per-room chance is not datamined (confidence: inferred).
